title: MCQ – Chapters 18 to 26 Mixed (200 Questions)
source: Short Textbook of Anesthesia – Ajay Yadav (6th Edition) – Chapters 18–26
section: Anesthesia for Coexisting Diseases (Cardiovascular, Respiratory, CNS, Hepatic, Renal, Endocrine, Neuromuscular, Immune, Blood Disorders)

questions.question: Effort tolerance > 4 METS is generally associated with:
questions.explanation: Effort tolerance > 4 METS is generally associated with lesser complications.

questions.question: Ideally smoking should be stopped before surgery:
questions.explanation: Ideally smoking should be stopped 8 weeks before surgery.

questions.question: Thiopentone is the induction agent of choice for:
questions.explanation: Thiopentone is the induction agent of choice for epileptics.

questions.question: Child-Pugh classification is the gold standard scoring system to assess severity of:
questions.explanation: Child-Pugh classification is the gold standard scoring system to assess the severity of hepatic disease.

questions.question: Dialysis should be performed within how many hours before surgery?
questions.explanation: Dialysis should be performed within 24 hours before surgery.

questions.question: The target plasma sugar level in perioperative period should be:
questions.explanation: The target plasma sugar level should be between 150–180 mg/dL in perioperative period.

questions.question: The anesthetic technique of choice for myasthenia gravis patients is:
questions.explanation: Local/regional anesthesia is the anesthetic technique of choice for myasthenia gravis patients.

questions.question: In rheumatoid arthritis, intubation should be performed with neck in:
questions.explanation: Due to atlantoaxial instability intubation in patients with rheumatoid arthritis should be performed with neck in extension.

questions.question: Minimum hemoglobin considered optimal for elective surgery is:
questions.explanation: Minimum hemoglobin of 8 g/dL is considered optimal to accept the patient for elective surgery.

questions.question: TEE is the earliest and most sensitive monitor to detect:
questions.explanation: Transesophageal echocardiography (TEE) is the earliest and most sensitive monitor to detect intra-operative infarction.

questions.question: Sevoflurane is the inhalational agent of choice for:
questions.explanation: Sevoflurane is the inhalational agent of choice for asthma patients.

questions.question: After cerebrovascular accident, elective surgery should be deferred for:
questions.explanation: After cerebrovascular accident elective surgery should be deferred for 9 months.

questions.question: The induction agent of choice for hepatic patients is:
questions.explanation: Propofol, due to its short half-life and extrahepatic metabolism is the induction agent of choice for hepatic patients.

questions.question: Desflurane is the inhalational agent of choice for renal patients because it:
questions.explanation: Desflurane is the inhalational agent of choice because it does not produce fluoride.

questions.question: Myocardial ischemia is the most common cause of death in perioperative period in:
questions.explanation: Myocardial ischemia is the most common cause of death in perioperative period in diabetic patients.

questions.question: Myasthenic patients are resistant to depolarizers and hypersensitive to:
questions.explanation: Myasthenic patients are resistant to depolarizers and hypersensitive to non-depolarizers.

questions.question: CD4 count and viral load do NOT determine the rate of:
questions.explanation: CD4 count and viral load do not determine the rate of perioperative anesthetic complications.

questions.question: The most important perioperative goal in sickle cell patients is to avoid:

options.B: Hypoxia, acidosis, dehydration, hypothermia, stress and pain

questions.explanation: The most important perioperative goal in sickle cell patients is to avoid hypoxia, acidosis, dehydration, hypothermia, stress and pain.

questions.question: Etomidate is the induction agent of choice for:
questions.explanation: Etomidate is the induction agent of choice for cardiac patients.

questions.question: Regional anesthesia is always preferred over general anesthesia for:
questions.explanation: Regional anesthesia is always preferred over general anesthesia for respiratory compromised patients.

questions.question: Levodopa should NOT be stopped before surgery in patients with:
questions.explanation: Levodopa should not be stopped before surgery.

questions.question: Sevoflurane is the inhalational agent of choice for hepatic patients while which agent should be avoided?
questions.explanation: Sevoflurane is the inhalational agent of choice for hepatic patients while halothane and nitrous oxide should be avoided.

questions.question: Sevoflurane should NOT be used in renal patients because it produces high levels of:
questions.explanation: Sevoflurane should not be used in renal patients as it produces high levels of fluoride approaching renal threshold of 50 μmol/L.

questions.question: Halothane is absolutely contraindicated in pheochromocytoma because it:
questions.explanation: Halothane is absolutely contraindicated in pheochromocytoma because it sensitizes myocardium to catecholamines.

questions.question: Succinylcholine is absolutely contraindicated for muscular dystrophies because it may produce:
questions.explanation: Succinylcholine is absolutely contraindicated for muscular dystrophies; it may produce cardiac arrest due to hyperkalemia and rhabdomyolysis.

questions.question: In ankylosing spondylitis, fusion of lumbar spine may make which procedure impossible?
questions.explanation: In patients with ankylosing spondylitis fusion of lumbar spine may make central neuraxial blocks impossible.

questions.question: Exchange transfusion for sickle cell patients is:
questions.explanation: Exchange transfusion is not practiced technique for sickle cell patient in current day.

questions.question: Vecuronium is the muscle relaxant of choice for:
questions.explanation: Vecuronium is the muscle relaxant of choice for cardiac patients.

questions.question: Thoracic epidural is the most preferred approach for analgesia in:
questions.explanation: Thoracic epidural is the most preferred approach to achieve analgesia for thoracic and upper abdominal surgeries.

questions.question: Spinal anesthesia can precipitate:
questions.explanation: Spinal anesthesia (but not epidural) and stress can precipitate multiple sclerosis.

questions.question: Atracurium and Cis-atracurium are the muscle relaxants of choice for:
questions.explanation: Atracurium and Cis-atracurium are the muscle relaxants of choice for hepatic patients.

questions.question: TURP syndrome is exhibited as fluid overloading, dilutional hyponatremia and:
questions.explanation: TURP syndrome is exhibited as fluid overloading, dilutional hyponatremia and hypo-osmolality.

questions.question: Regular insulin by continuous infusion is the most recommended method to control:
questions.explanation: Regular insulin by continuous infusion is the most recommended method to control intraoperative plasma glucose.

questions.question: Desflurane is the most preferred inhalational agent for myasthenia gravis because it produces:
questions.explanation: Desflurane is the most preferred inhalational agent for maintenance in myasthenia gravis patients because it produces maximum muscle relaxation.

questions.question: Regional anesthesia is contraindicated in patients with:
questions.explanation: Regional anesthesia is contraindicated for patients with deficient coagulation factor disorders like hemophilia.

questions.question: CNB should be avoided for fixed cardiac output lesions like:
questions.explanation: Central neuraxial blocks (CNB) should be avoided for fixed cardiac output lesions like mitral or aortic stenosis.

questions.question: Intraoperative bronchospasm should first be treated by:

options.B: Increasing depth of anesthesia with inhalational agent

questions.explanation: Intraoperative bronchospasm should first be treated by increasing the depth of anesthesia with inhalational agent (preferably sevoflurane).

questions.question: Methohexital is the induction agent of choice for:
questions.explanation: Methohexital is the induction agent of choice for electroconvulsive therapy.

questions.question: Albumin is the preferred colloid for patients with compromised:
questions.explanation: Albumin is the preferred colloid for a patient with compromised hepatic functions.

questions.question: Non-depolarizing relaxant of choice for renal failure is:
questions.explanation: Non-depolarizing relaxant of choice is atracurium/Cis-atracurium.

questions.question: Thiopentone is the induction agent of choice in:
questions.explanation: Thiopentone is the induction agent of choice in hyperthyroid patients.

questions.question: Myasthenic syndrome patients are sensitive to:

options.C: Both depolarizing and non-depolarizing muscle relaxants

questions.explanation: Myasthenic syndrome patients are sensitive to both depolarizing and non-depolarizing muscle relaxants.

questions.question: Platelet count recommended before considering patients for surgery is more than:
questions.explanation: Platelet count of > 50,000/mm3 is recommended before considering the patients for surgery.

questions.question: Beta blockers should not be started afresh unless they can be started at least:
questions.explanation: Beta blockers should not be started afresh unless they can be started at least 1 week before surgery.

questions.question: Ketamine for asthma patients is restricted to:
questions.explanation: Ketamine use is restricted to patients in active asthma (wheezing) undergoing life-threatening emergency surgeries.

questions.question: Dopamine antagonists like droperidol and metoclopramide should NOT be used in:
questions.explanation: Dopamine antagonists like droperidol and metoclopramide should not be used in patients with Parkinson's disease.

questions.question: Due to existing coagulopathy and intravascular volume depletion, CNB should be avoided in:
questions.explanation: Due to the possibility of existing coagulopathy and intravascular volume depletion, central neuraxial blocks should be avoided in hepatic patients.

questions.question: Suxamethonium is contraindicated in renal patients due to:
questions.explanation: Suxamethonium is contraindicated due to associated hyperkalemia.

questions.question: Etomidate is contraindicated in adrenal insufficiency because it causes:
questions.explanation: Etomidate is contraindicated in adrenal insufficiency because it causes adrenocortical suppression.

questions.question: Halothane is contraindicated in muscular dystrophies because it can precipitate:
questions.explanation: Halothane is contraindicated in muscular dystrophies as it can precipitate malignant hyperthermia.

questions.question: The deficit coagulation factor should be increased to at least what percentage before surgery?
questions.explanation: The deficit coagulation factor should be increased to at least 50% of the normal before considering the patient for surgery.

questions.question: The most important goal of anesthetic management for ischemic heart disease is to avoid imbalance between:
questions.explanation: The most important goal is to avoid an imbalance between myocardial oxygen demand and supply.

questions.question: Thiopentone can induce bronchoconstriction therefore should be avoided in:
questions.explanation: Thiopentone can induce bronchoconstriction therefore, should be avoided.

questions.question: Regional anesthesia and deep GA can prevent:
questions.explanation: Regional anesthesia and deep GA can prevent autonomic hyper-reflexia.

questions.question: Vecuronium should be avoided for patients with biliary obstruction because it has 30-40% excretion through:
questions.explanation: As vecuronium has 30–40% excretion through bile, it should be avoided for patients with biliary obstruction.

questions.question: If invasive BP monitoring is required in renal patients, radial and ulnar artery should be spared for future:
questions.explanation: Radial and ulnar artery should be spared as they may be required for hemodialysis in future.

questions.question: At least tachycardia should be settled in which patients undergoing emergency surgery?
questions.explanation: At least tachycardia should be settled in hyperthyroid patients undergoing emergency surgery.

questions.question: Safe non-depolarizers for myasthenia gravis include mivacurium, atracurium and:
questions.explanation: Mivacurium, atracurium, and Cis-atracurium are safe non-depolarizers for myasthenia gravis patients.

questions.question: Intravenous regional anesthesia (Bier's block) can be utilized for:
questions.explanation: Intravenous regional anesthesia (Bier's block) can be utilized for sickle cell patients.

questions.question: If patient had MI and was treated with conventional drug eluting stent, elective surgery must be deferred for:
questions.explanation: Elective surgery must be deferred for 1 year if conventional drug eluting stent was used.

questions.question: Propofol is preferred over ketamine for asthma patients in:
questions.explanation: Propofol is preferred over ketamine for asthma patients in present day anesthesia practice.

questions.question: All antiepileptics should be:
questions.explanation: All antiepileptic should be continued.

questions.question: Elective surgery should be deferred in acute liver disease till:
questions.explanation: Elective surgery should be deferred in acute liver disease till the liver functions are stabilized.

questions.question: Correction of which electrolyte is of utmost importance in CKD patients?
questions.explanation: Correction of hyperkalemia is of utmost importance in chronic kidney disease patients.

questions.question: Ruling out autonomic neuropathy is most important in preoperative assessment of:
questions.explanation: Ruling out autonomic neuropathy is one of the most important part of preoperative assessment in diabetic patient.

questions.question: For myasthenic patients, anticholinesterases should be:
questions.explanation: For myasthenic patients anticholinesterases should be continued in reduced doses.

questions.question: In ankylosing spondylitis, fusion of cervical spine may make which procedure impossible?
questions.explanation: Fusion of cervical spine may make intubation impossible.

questions.question: Target hematocrit for sickle cell patients before surgery should be more than:
questions.explanation: The approach is to correct the anemia with target hematocrit > 30 (Hemoglobin >10 g%).

questions.question: Cardiac patients with compromised LV function should preferably be maintained with:
questions.explanation: If left ventricular function is compromised then anaesthesia should preferably be maintained with opioids.

questions.question: Nitrous oxide should not be used in:
questions.explanation: Nitrous oxide should not be used in emphysema.

questions.question: Acute drug intake decreases while chronic intake increases:
questions.explanation: Acute drug intake decreases while chronic intake increases the anesthetic requirement.

questions.question: Propofol is preferred for hepatic patients due to short half-life and:
questions.explanation: Propofol, due to its short half-life and extrahepatic metabolism is the induction agent of choice for hepatic patients.

questions.question: Ringer lactate is preferred over normal saline for renal patients with close monitoring of:
questions.explanation: Ringer lactate is preferred over normal saline with close monitoring of potassium levels.

questions.question: Elective surgery should be deferred in hyper- or hypothyroid patients till patient is:
questions.explanation: Elective surgery should be deferred till the patient is euthyroid.

questions.question: Non-depolarizers in muscular dystrophies show increased sensitivity; safer choices include mivacurium, atracurium and:
questions.explanation: Non-depolarizers exhibit increased sensitivity making mivacurium, atracurium, cis-atracurium safer choices.

questions.question: Barbiturates are absolutely contraindicated for all porphyrias EXCEPT:
questions.explanation: Barbiturates are absolutely contraindicated for all kind of porphyrias except porphyria cutanea tarda and erythropoietic protoporphyrias.

questions.question: If a patient with drug eluting stent has to undergo emergency surgery, the preferred approach is:
questions.explanation: If a patient with drug eluted stent has to undergo emergency surgery then continuing antiplatelet therapy is always preferred.

questions.question: For URTI patient, GA with LMA is preferred over:
questions.explanation: For patient of URTI general anesthesia (GA) with laryngeal mask airway (LMA) is always preferred over GA with intubation.

questions.question: Patients must NOT be taken for elective surgeries in:
questions.explanation: Patients must not be taken for elective surgeries in acute drug withdrawal.

questions.question: Halothane and nitrous oxide should be avoided in:
questions.explanation: Halothane and nitrous oxide should be avoided in hepatic patients.

questions.question: Hypermagnesemia potentiates the action of:

options.C: Both depolarizing and non-depolarizing muscle relaxants

questions.explanation: Hypermagnesemia potentiates the action of both depolarizing and non-depolarizing muscle relaxants.

questions.question: As metabolism of drugs is reduced in hypothyroidism, all drugs should be used in:
questions.explanation: As the metabolism of drugs is reduced in hypothyroidism, all drugs should be used in minimal possible doses.

questions.question: Myasthenic patients are resistant to which type of muscle relaxants?
questions.explanation: Myasthenic patients are resistant to depolarizers.

questions.question: Propofol, opioids and suxamethonium are considered safe for:
questions.explanation: Propofol, opioids and suxamethonium are considered safe for porphyrias patients.

questions.question: CPR may be ineffective in patients with severe:
questions.explanation: CPR may be ineffective in patients with severe aortic stenosis.

questions.question: Elective surgery should be deferred in patients with active:
questions.explanation: Elective surgery should be deferred in a patient of active (open) pulmonary tuberculosis.

questions.question: All antidepressants including MAO inhibitors should be:
questions.explanation: All antidepressants including monoamine oxidase (MAO) inhibitors should be continued.

questions.question: Elective surgery is contraindicated in patients with:
questions.explanation: Elective surgery is contraindicated in patients with lower respiratory tract infection (lung infections).

questions.question: Patients with acute kidney injury should not undergo elective surgery until:
questions.explanation: Patients with acute kidney injury (AKI) should not undergo elective surgery until the injury is fully resolved.

questions.question: Increase in catecholamines during tumor handling can cause profound hypertension in:
questions.explanation: Increase in catecholamines during tumor handling can cause profound hypertension and life-threatening arrhythmias during pheochromocytoma surgery.

questions.question: Ketamine increases systemic vascular resistance making it agent of choice for:
questions.explanation: As ketamine increases the systemic vascular resistance it is agent of choice for right to left shunts.

questions.question: Ventilation principle for COPD/asthma includes low tidal volume and slow respiratory rate to prevent:
questions.explanation: Low tidal volume, low I:E ratio and slow respiratory rate to allow adequate exhalation and prevent air trapping.

questions.question: Current recommendation is to continue:
questions.explanation: Current recommendation is to continue lithium.

questions.question: CNB should be avoided in renal patients due to uremic coagulopathy and:
questions.explanation: Central neuraxial blocks should be avoided due to associated uremic coagulopathy, uremic polyneuropathy and intolerance of renal patients to extra fluids.

questions.question: Mild hypothyroidism should NOT be a deterrent to:
questions.explanation: Mild hypothyroidism should not be a deterrent to postpone the surgery.

questions.question: For myasthenia gravis, the most preferred inhalational agent is desflurane because of:
questions.explanation: Desflurane produces maximum muscle relaxation among the currently used inhalational agents.

questions.question: Fasting, dehydration and infection can trigger acute crisis of:
questions.explanation: Fasting, dehydration and infection can trigger acute crisis of porphyria.

questions.question: For bare metal stent, elective surgery must be deferred for:
questions.explanation: Elective surgery must be deferred for 6 weeks if bare metal stent was used.

questions.question: If surgery has to be deferred in a patient of URTI, it should ideally be deferred for:
questions.explanation: If surgery has to be deferred in a patient of URTI then it should ideally be deferred for 6 weeks.

questions.question: The rising concern that general anesthesia worsens dementia is:

options.B: Not yet fully substantiated by studies

questions.explanation: The rising concern that general anesthesia worsens the dementia is not yet fully substantiated by studies.

questions.question: Basic aim for Left to Right Shunts is to maintain:
questions.explanation: For Left to Right Shunts: maintain low vascular resistance and maintain high pulmonary vascular resistance.

questions.question: Parenteral opioids should be avoided as much as possible for thoracic and upper abdominal surgeries; instead use:
questions.explanation: Thoracic epidural is most preferred while parenteral opioids should be avoided as much as possible.

questions.question: Elective surgery should be deferred after MI for 6 weeks if patient was treated with:
questions.explanation: Deferred for 6 weeks if treated without stenting, for 6 weeks with bare metal stent, for 6 weeks if treated with CABG.

questions.question: Colloids (hydroxyethyl starch) have been found to exacerbate:
questions.explanation: Colloids (hydroxyethyl starch) have been found to exacerbate renal injury.

questions.question: Administrating methylene blue can be life-threatening in patients with:
questions.explanation: Administrating methylene blue can be life-threatening in patients with G6PD deficiency.

questions.question: Aspirin should be continued while which drug should be stopped 5 days before surgery?
questions.explanation: Aspirin should be continued while Clopidogrel should be stopped 5 days before surgery.

questions.question: Routine use of pulmonary function tests for preoperative assessment is:
questions.explanation: The routine use of pulmonary function tests is not recommended.

questions.question: Perioperative goal for Right to Left Shunts is to maintain:
questions.explanation: For Right to Left Shunts: maintain high vascular resistance (hypertension) or decrease pulmonary vascular resistance.

questions.question: Hyperkalemia must be corrected before surgery while mild chronic hypokalemia:
questions.explanation: Hyperkalemia must be corrected before taking the patient for surgery while there is no need to correct mild chronic hypokalemia.

questions.question: Stopping smoking 12 hours before surgery may be beneficial by reducing:
questions.explanation: Stopping smoking 12 hours before may be beneficial by reducing carboxyhemoglobin levels.

questions.question: Cardiac patients with normal LV function can safely receive:
questions.explanation: Cardiac patients with normal left ventricular function can safely receive inhalational agents.

questions.question: Always rule out associated cor pulmonale in patients with:
questions.explanation: Always rule out associated cor pulmonale in patients with pulmonary disease.

questions.question: Dyspnea at minimal activity and site of surgery are the two most important predictors of:
questions.explanation: Dyspnea at minimal activity and site of surgery are the two most important predictors of postoperative complications.

questions.question: For new generation drug eluting stent, elective surgery must be deferred for:
questions.explanation: Elective surgery must be deferred for 6 months if new generation drug eluting stent was used.

questions.question: Ventilation principle for COPD includes respiratory rate of:
questions.explanation: Slow respiratory rate of 6–10 breaths/min to allow adequate exhalation.

questions.question: Antibiotics prophylaxis for prosthetic heart valve patients is now recommended only for:
questions.explanation: New guidelines recommend antibiotics prophylaxis only for dental procedures involving gingiva, oral mucosa and periapical region.

questions.question: Elective surgery after MI with CABG should be deferred for:
questions.explanation: Deferred for 6 weeks if treated with coronary artery bypass graft (CABG).

questions.question: Maintaining fluid balance in renal patients is essential because excess fluid can precipitate:
questions.explanation: Excess fluid can precipitate pulmonary edema and fluid deficiency can worsen kidney injury.

questions.question: Propofol is preferred for asthma patients in present day practice over:
questions.explanation: Propofol is preferred over ketamine for asthma patients in present day anesthesia practice.

questions.question: Two most important predictors of postoperative respiratory complications are dyspnea at minimal activity and:
questions.explanation: Dyspnea at minimal activity and site of surgery are the two most important predictors.

questions.question: For diabetic patients, autonomic neuropathy assessment is important because it affects:
questions.explanation: Ruling out autonomic neuropathy is one of the most important part of preoperative assessment in diabetic patient.

questions.question: Tidal volume for COPD/asthma patients during anesthesia should be:
questions.explanation: Low tidal volume (6–8 mL/kg) for COPD/asthma patients.

questions.question: Myasthenic crisis can occur if anticholinesterases are:
questions.explanation: Discontinuation increases the possibility of myasthenic crisis, therefore continue with adjustments.

questions.question: Sickle cell patients should be given red cell transfusion with target hemoglobin of:
questions.explanation: Correct the anemia by giving red cell transfusion with target Hemoglobin >10 g%.

questions.question: The approach in present day practice towards blood products is:
questions.explanation: The approach in present day practice is towards the minimum use of blood products called as restrictive approach to transfusion.

questions.question: Sevoflurane for hepatic patients is preferred because it maintains:
questions.explanation: Hepatic blood flow is best maintained with sevoflurane.

questions.question: Child-Pugh classification assesses severity based on bilirubin, albumin, PT, ascites and:
questions.explanation: Child-Pugh classification uses bilirubin, albumin, PT, ascites and encephalopathy grade.

questions.question: For renal patients, colloids should be avoided because they can cause:
questions.explanation: Colloids (particularly hydroxyethyl starch) cause renal dysfunction and therefore must not be used.

questions.question: Normal saline in renal patients can cause hyperchloremic acidosis leading to:
questions.explanation: Normal saline by causing hyperchloremic acidosis can cause hyperkalemia therefore should not be used.

questions.question: Perioperative fluid therapy in hepatic patients should be guided by:
questions.explanation: Fluids should be guided by cardiac monitoring.

questions.question: Ketamine increases SVR and is agent of choice for induction in:
questions.explanation: As ketamine increases the systemic vascular resistance it is agent of choice for right to left shunts.

questions.question: Elective surgery is contraindicated in lower respiratory tract infection but decision for URTI depends on:
questions.explanation: The decision to go ahead with elective surgery in patient with URTI depends on the severity of the disease.

questions.question: Spinal anesthesia can precipitate MS but which regional technique is safe?
questions.explanation: Spinal anesthesia (but not epidural) can precipitate multiple sclerosis; peripheral blocks can be given safely.

questions.question: In hypothyroidism, all drugs should be used in minimal doses because:
questions.explanation: As the metabolism of drugs is reduced in hypothyroidism, all drugs should be used in minimal possible doses.

questions.question: Myasthenic syndrome (Eaton-Lambert) patients are sensitive to:
questions.explanation: Myasthenic syndrome patients are sensitive to both depolarizing and non-depolarizing muscle relaxants.

questions.question: Pheochromocytoma surgery — halothane is contraindicated while the major perioperative concern is:
questions.explanation: Increase in catecholamines during tumor handling can cause profound hypertension and life-threatening arrhythmias.

questions.question: Albumin is preferred colloid for hepatic patients because other colloids may worsen:
questions.explanation: Albumin is the preferred colloid for hepatic patients.

questions.question: Succinylcholine in muscular dystrophies can cause rhabdomyolysis and precipitate:
questions.explanation: Succinylcholine can precipitate malignant hyperthermia in muscular dystrophy patients.

questions.question: TURP syndrome treatment includes stopping irrigation, diuretics, and correction of:
questions.explanation: TURP syndrome is exhibited as dilutional hyponatremia — correction of hyponatremia is key.

questions.question: Desflurane for renal patients is preferred over sevoflurane because desflurane:
questions.explanation: Desflurane is the inhalational agent of choice because it does not produce fluoride.

questions.question: For cardiac patients with left to right shunts, the aim is to maintain:
questions.explanation: For Left to Right Shunts: maintain low vascular resistance (hypotension).

questions.question: Sevoflurane produces fluoride approaching renal threshold of:
questions.explanation: Sevoflurane produces high levels of fluoride approaching renal threshold of 50 μmol/L.

questions.question: Dopamine antagonists are contraindicated in Parkinson's because they worsen:
questions.explanation: Dopamine antagonists worsen Parkinson's symptoms by further reducing dopamine activity.

questions.question: For hepatic patients, which inhalational agent should specifically be avoided?
questions.explanation: Halothane should be avoided in hepatic patients.

questions.question: Autonomic hyper-reflexia can be prevented by:
questions.explanation: Regional anesthesia and deep GA can prevent autonomic hyper-reflexia.

questions.question: TEE is earliest and most sensitive for detecting intraoperative MI. The induction agent of choice for cardiac patients is:
questions.explanation: Etomidate is the induction agent of choice for cardiac patients.

questions.question: Target plasma glucose during perioperative period for diabetics should NOT exceed:
questions.explanation: Target should be between 150–180 mg/dL.

questions.question: Renal threshold for fluoride toxicity is:
questions.explanation: Renal threshold beyond which fluoride levels are toxic is 50 μmol/L.

questions.question: After cerebrovascular accident, carotid endarterectomy can be undertaken after:
questions.explanation: Carotid endarterectomy can be undertaken after 6 weeks.

questions.question: Restrictive approach to transfusion means:
questions.explanation: The approach is towards the minimum use of blood products called as restrictive approach.

questions.question: Which agents are safe for porphyria patients?
questions.explanation: Propofol, opioids and suxamethonium are considered safe for porphyrias patients.

questions.question: Thiopentone is preferred for hyperthyroid patients because it has:
questions.explanation: Thiopentone has anti-thyroid properties making it agent of choice for hyperthyroid patients.

questions.question: Vecuronium is muscle relaxant of choice for cardiac patients due to:
questions.explanation: Vecuronium is cardiovascular stable making it the choice for cardiac patients.

questions.question: Inhalational agent of choice for hepatic patients that does NOT produce trifluoroacetic acid is:
questions.explanation: Sevoflurane does not produce trifluoroacetic acid, therefore cannot cause immunologic hepatitis.

questions.question: Atracurium/Cis-atracurium are choice for renal failure because they undergo:
questions.explanation: Atracurium/Cis-atracurium are metabolized by Hoffman degradation — organ-independent elimination.

questions.question: Intraoperative bronchospasm treatment: first step is increase depth of anesthesia, preferred agent is:
questions.explanation: Increase depth with inhalational agent — preferably sevoflurane.

questions.question: Muscular dystrophy patients — both halothane and succinylcholine should be avoided to prevent:
questions.explanation: Both can precipitate malignant hyperthermia in muscular dystrophies.

questions.question: For left to right shunts: maintain low SVR. For right to left shunts: maintain:
questions.explanation: For Right to Left Shunts: maintain high vascular resistance (hypertension).

questions.question: G6PD deficiency — methylene blue is contraindicated because it can be:
questions.explanation: Administrating methylene blue can be life-threatening in patients with G6PD deficiency.

questions.question: For sickle cell patients, Bier's block:
questions.explanation: Intravenous regional anesthesia (Bier's block) can be utilized for sickle cell patients.

questions.question: Atlantoaxial instability requiring neck extension during intubation is seen in:
questions.explanation: Due to atlantoaxial instability intubation in patients with rheumatoid arthritis should be performed with neck in extension.

questions.question: Nitrous oxide avoidance in emphysema is because it can:
questions.explanation: Nitrous oxide can expand air-filled cavities including emphysematous bullae.

questions.question: Myasthenia gravis — desflurane produces maximum muscle relaxation. This means less need for:
questions.explanation: Maximum muscle relaxation from desflurane means less need for muscle relaxants in myasthenia patients.

questions.question: CNB should be avoided in hepatic patients due to existing:
questions.explanation: Due to existing coagulopathy, central neuraxial blocks should be avoided in hepatic patients.

questions.question: Acute drug intake (intoxication) on anesthetic requirement:
questions.explanation: Acute drug intake decreases the anesthetic requirement.

questions.question: Chronic drug intake on anesthetic requirement:
questions.explanation: Chronic intake increases the anesthetic requirement.

questions.question: Cardiopulmonary bypass patients with prosthetic valves — new guidelines for antibiotic prophylaxis cover only:

options.B: Dental procedures and invasive procedures on infective tissue

questions.explanation: New guidelines recommend antibiotics prophylaxis only for dental procedures and invasive procedures on infective tissue.

questions.question: Thoracic epidural provides better analgesia than parenteral opioids and also reduces:
questions.explanation: Thoracic epidural is most preferred for thoracic surgeries reducing respiratory complications.

questions.question: The gold standard for hepatic risk assessment is:
questions.explanation: Child-Pugh classification is the gold standard scoring system.

questions.question: Etomidate for adrenal insufficiency patients is:
questions.explanation: Etomidate is contraindicated in adrenal insufficiency because it causes adrenocortical suppression.

questions.question: In renal patients, which crystalloid is preferred with potassium monitoring?
questions.explanation: Ringer lactate is preferred over normal saline with close monitoring of potassium levels.

questions.question: The induction agent of choice for ECT (electroconvulsive therapy) is:
questions.explanation: Methohexital is the induction agent of choice for electroconvulsive therapy.

questions.question: Sevoflurane for asthma provides excellent:
questions.explanation: Excellent bronchodilatation makes Sevoflurane the inhalational agent of choice for asthmatics.

questions.question: Atracurium and Cis-atracurium are muscle relaxants of choice for hepatic patients because of:
questions.explanation: They are metabolized by Hoffman degradation — organ-independent.

questions.question: Myasthenic patients need reduced doses of non-depolarizers because they are:
questions.explanation: Myasthenic patients are hypersensitive to non-depolarizers.

questions.question: Regional anesthesia is contraindicated in hemophilia because of:
questions.explanation: Regional anesthesia is contraindicated for patients with deficient coagulation factor disorders like hemophilia.

questions.question: CNB is contraindicated for fixed cardiac output lesions because hypotension from sympathetic block cannot be compensated by:
questions.explanation: Fixed output lesions (stenosis) cannot increase CO to compensate for SVR drop from CNB.

questions.question: Levodopa withdrawal can cause:
questions.explanation: Withdrawal of levodopa can cause muscular rigidity.

questions.question: Propofol for hepatic patients is preferred over thiopentone because of:
questions.explanation: Propofol, due to its short half-life and extrahepatic metabolism is the induction agent of choice.

questions.question: Fluoride levels produced by sevoflurane approach toxic threshold of 50 μmol/L making it unsuitable for:
questions.explanation: Sevoflurane should not be used in renal patients as it produces high levels of fluoride.

questions.question: Effort tolerance assessment: 4 METS is equivalent to:
questions.explanation: 4 METS is generally equivalent to climbing 1 flight of stairs without stopping.

questions.question: Porphyria crisis can be triggered by fasting. Therefore preoperative fasting should be:
questions.explanation: Fasting can trigger acute crisis of porphyria, so minimize fasting with IV dextrose support.

questions.question: For asthma patients, thiopentone should be avoided because it can cause:
questions.explanation: Thiopentone can induce bronchoconstriction therefore should be avoided.

questions.question: Elective surgery should be deferred in acute liver disease until:
questions.explanation: Elective surgery should be deferred in acute liver disease till the liver functions are stabilized.

questions.question: Diabetic autonomic neuropathy can affect response to:
questions.explanation: Autonomic neuropathy affects hemodynamic response making its assessment critical preoperatively.

questions.question: For sickle cell patients, perioperative goals include avoiding all EXCEPT:
questions.explanation: Avoid hypoxia, acidosis, dehydration, hypothermia, stress and pain — normothermia is the goal, not something to avoid.

questions.question: Vecuronium has 30-40% biliary excretion so it should be avoided in:
questions.explanation: Vecuronium has 30–40% excretion through bile, it should be avoided for patients with biliary obstruction.

questions.question: Non-depolarizers show increased sensitivity in muscular dystrophies. The safest choices are atracurium, cis-atracurium and:
questions.explanation: Mivacurium, atracurium, cis-atracurium are safer choices in muscular dystrophies.

questions.question: TEE detects intraoperative MI by showing:
questions.explanation: TEE detects ischemia/infarction by detecting wall motion abnormality.

questions.question: For respiratory compromised patients, the preferred anesthesia technique is:
questions.explanation: Regional anesthesia is always preferred over general anesthesia for respiratory compromised patients.

questions.question: Hyperkalemia in CKD patients — correction before surgery is of utmost importance because it can cause:
questions.explanation: Hyperkalemia can cause fatal cardiac arrhythmias, hence correction is utmost importance.

questions.question: Catecholamine surge during pheochromocytoma surgery is managed by:
questions.explanation: Alpha blockade followed by beta blockade is the perioperative management for pheochromocytoma.

questions.question: Myasthenia gravis — anticholinesterases should be continued in reduced doses to avoid:
questions.explanation: Continue in reduced doses — stopping causes myasthenic crisis, overdose causes cholinergic crisis.

questions.question: Ankylosing spondylitis — lumbar spine fusion makes spinal/epidural impossible and cervical fusion makes intubation:
questions.explanation: Fusion of lumbar spine may make CNB impossible and fusion of cervical spine may make intubation impossible.

questions.question: Sickle cell patients — avoid hypothermia because cold can trigger:
questions.explanation: Hypothermia can trigger sickling — must be avoided.

questions.question: Normal saline should NOT be used in renal patients because hyperchloremic acidosis worsens:
questions.explanation: Normal saline causing hyperchloremic acidosis can worsen hyperkalemia.

questions.question: Cardiac patients with fixed output lesions (stenosis) — avoid CNB because:

options.B: Sympathetic block causes hypotension that cannot be compensated

questions.explanation: CNB should be avoided for fixed cardiac output lesions like mitral or aortic stenosis.

questions.question: Elective surgery after MI without stenting should be deferred for:
questions.explanation: Elective surgery must be deferred for 6 weeks if the patient was treated without stenting.

questions.question: For hepatic patients, nitrous oxide should be avoided because it can worsen:
questions.explanation: Nitrous oxide should be avoided in hepatic patients (can expand gas-filled spaces).

questions.question: The most common cause of perioperative death in diabetic patients is:
questions.explanation: Myocardial ischemia is the most common cause of death in perioperative period in diabetic patients.
